channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Review: The Machines Have Come for the Children
item.description: In this latest installment of the popular franchise, Woody and Buzz Lightyear are back and must contend with Bonnie’s mesmerizing new toy.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: ‘The Death of Robin Hood’ Review: Not a Merry Band
item.description: A darkly revisionist take on the medieval outlaw asks who gets to craft the heroic legends.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: ‘Maddie’s Secret’ Review: She’s Got the Sauce
item.description: A food influencer struggles with an eating disorder in John Early’s thrilling pastiche of the made-for-TV melodrama.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: ‘Leviticus’ Review: The Shocking Shapes That Love Can Take
item.description: In this Australian chiller, a conversion therapy ritual unleashes shape-shifting ghouls that take the form of desired ones.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: ‘Girls Like Girls’ Review: Hayley Kiyoko Brings Her Song to Life
item.description: The pop star Hayley Kiyoko turns her hit single and best-selling book into a coming-of-age romance.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: Emily Blunt on ‘Disclosure Day’ and Feeling Flappable Around Spielberg
item.description: The star grew up on the director’s films and tried “to really compose myself” when he invited her to a meeting about a secret movie.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I remember being completely traumatized by ‘Jaws’ and thrilled by ‘Indiana Jones,’” Emily Blunt said. “So it is a very surreal thing” when Steven Spielberg wants to work with you.

item.title: How ‘The Furious’ Somersaults Through a Wild Action Scene
item.description: The new movie is packed with showstopping action, but one standout sequence has our hero rolling over a pyramid of henchmen. Here’s what it took.
item.dc:creator: Mekado Murphy

item.title: At the Canadian Screen Awards, the Industry Steps Outside Hollywood’s Shadow
item.description: No longer content with being just “Hollywood North,” the Canadian screen industry is asserting itself and telling its own stories.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Cole Burston
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actor Robbie Graham-Kunz speaking with fans outside the 14th Canadian Screen Awards in Toronto.

item.title: Steven Spielberg on How to Build Suspense in ‘Disclosure Day’
item.description: The director narrates a scene from his movie that is a continuation, of sorts, of a sequence he filmed for his feature-length debut, “Duel,” in 1971.
item.dc:creator: Mekado Murphy

item.title: San Francisco Film Patrons Are Found Dead on Side of Highway
item.description: Judith Sheldon, 84, daughter of an Oscar-winning director, and her husband, Wylie, 86, were traveling to Oregon on a hot day. Their deaths are under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Madaleine Rubin

item.title: ‘Voicemails for Isabelle’ Review: An Unexpected Recipient
item.description: The movie, starring Zoey Deutch and Nick Robinson, begins as a tear-jerker and morphs into a poignant rom-com.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: ‘Unidentified’ Review: A High-Concept Murder Mystery
item.description: The director Haifaa Al-Mansour (“Wadjda”) concludes a trilogy about women resisting societal constraints in Saudi Arabia with a whodunit.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘Rose of Nevada’ Review: Out of the Mists of Time and Back Again
item.description: In this richly textured film by Mark Jenkin, two men sign on to crew a ship whose ultimate destination is a place they could never have fathomed.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: ‘Color Book’ Review: Love and Grieving
item.description: In this tender family drama, a widowed father and his disabled son overcome personal tragedy.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: ‘Never Change!’ Review: Failing the Assignment
item.description: This comedy squanders a promising premise about adults forced back to high school on repetitive sketches and hollow jokes.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: ‘The Devil Queen’ Review: A Seedy Quest for Vengeance
item.description: Antonio Carlos da Fontoura’s 1974 gay gangster thriller returns in a gorgeous new restoration.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg

item.title: Critics of Russia Say This Critic Isn’t Critical Enough
item.description: Alexander Sokurov has questioned the Russian president about government repression, but he is still controversial among his country’s exiles.
item.dc:creator: Milana Mazaeva and Neil MacFarquhar
item.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
item.media:description: Alexander Sokurov during the screening of his latest film, “Director’s Diary,” in Paris, in March.

item.title: Daveigh Chase, ‘Lilo & Stitch’ Voice Actor and ‘The Ring’ Villain, Dies at 35
item.description: She brought the plucky Lilo to life in the popular animated film series, and unnerved viewers as the child villain Samara in the horror film “The Ring.”
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:description: Daveigh Chase in 2011. Her father said she had struggled with drugs since the age of 13.

item.title: ‘Curse of the Seven Jackals’: A Film Made to Be Exhumed
item.description: Anthology Film Archives is screening Chris Jolly’s restored 2001 lo-fi indie film twice nightly.
item.dc:creator: J. Hoberman
item.media:description: Bernard (Kevin Barnes from the band Of Montreal) signs on to test an experimental form of synthetic blood.

item.title: ‘Are You Now or Have You Ever Been’ Review: Who Is Naming Names?
item.description: Anna D. Shapiro revives Eric Bentley’s play about the House Un-American Activities Committee’s investigation of the entertainment industry.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes
item.category: Are You Now or Have You Ever Been (Play)

item.title: The ‘Paddington’ Musical Will Come to Broadway Next Spring
item.description: The show, which revisits the story of a marmalade-loving bear, plans to open next April at the Hirschfeld Theater in New York.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Kalpesh Lathigra for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Paddington,” according to the musical’s producer Sonia Friedman, is “a show about fun, adventure, joy and comedy, but on a more profound level it’s a story about searching for home.”

item.title: Jessie Buckley, a Liza Minnelli Tribute and More Theater to Stream
item.description: The Oscar winner and Josh O’Connor star in a National Theater production of Shakespeare’s tragedy. Other picks include Matthew López’s “The Inheritance.”
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

item.title: Millennial Food Obsession Is a Joke. John Early Gets It.
item.description: In his new film, “Maddie’s Secret,” the comedian goes all in on earnestness, playing a cooking content creator.
item.dc:creator: Priya Krishna
item.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times

item.title: Seth Rogen Knows the Secret to Marriage — and Being Rich in Hollywood
item.description: After three decades in the industry, the actor-writer-director-producer has figured a lot out.
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
item.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: The Violence in Iran Was Real. The Film Showing It Was A.I.
item.description: The docudrama “Dreams of Violets” premiered at the Tribeca Festival, a sign that some in Hollywood have warmed to the technology.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez and Matt Stevens

item.title: Spielberg Gets Paranoid With ‘Disclosure Day’
item.description: Disclosure Day shows that after 50 years Spielberg’s fixations are the same, but his faith in the system has deteriorated.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson, Edward Vega and Alex Gallitano

item.title: Blake Lively Awarded Legal Fees in Ruling After Justin Baldoni Settlement
item.description: The size of the award remains to be determined. It only involves the legal fees associated with part of her court battle with Mr. Baldoni, with whom she made a film.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: A judge on Friday awarded Blake Lively some legal fees in her court battle with Justin Baldoni. The amount was not set in the ruling.

item.title: Five Action Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include a wartime rescue, teenage assassins and Emma Thompson’s rescue skills.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels
item.media:description: A still from “Beast,” featuring Daniel MacPherson.

item.title: Rick Jackson, Georgia Governor Candidate, Is Also a Film Producer Battling the IRS
item.description: Rick Jackson, a billionaire, wants to be Georgia’s next governor. But the I.R.S. has questioned how his low-budget movies turned into tax windfalls.
item.dc:creator: Danny Hakim
item.media:credit: Dustin Chambers for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rick Jackson has sought $90 million in tax deductions from four money-losing films.

item.title: 7 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: ‘Disclosure Day’ | Anatomy of a Scene
item.description: The director Steven Spielberg breaks down an action sequence from his new film “Disclosure Day” for Anatomy of a Scene.
item.dc:creator: Mekado Murphy, Sutton Raphael, Gabriel Blanco and Edward Vega

item.title: ‘The Gas Station Attendant’ Review: A Daughter’s Questions
item.description: Karla Murthy retells her Indian dad’s life story to better understand her own in this deeply personal documentary.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson
